expectations.text: Response contains at least one question mark — Claude asks for clarification rather than generating immediately
expectations.evidence: Five numbered questions all ending with question marks, asked before any update is generated.

expectations.text: Questions ask about project name or project context
expectations.evidence: Question 1: 'Project name — what's the project called?'

expectations.text: Questions ask about which sources to use (git, Slack, Jira, Notion, docs)
expectations.evidence: Question 4 explicitly lists all five source types: Git repo, Slack, Jira, Notion, local docs/ folder.

expectations.text: Questions ask about the time period to cover
expectations.evidence: Question 3: 'Time period — what window should it cover? (e.g. "this week", "last sprint", "since last Monday")'

expectations.text: All clarifying questions are grouped into a single message (not spread across multiple turns)
expectations.evidence: All 5 questions appear in one block under 'Claude's initial questions (Step 1)', introduced with 'I'll ask everything at once'.

expectations.text: Output includes a screenshot or image section referencing the provided file or noting it for attachment
expectations.evidence: Has '📸 *Screenshot:* New login flow UI...' section at the bottom with the file path docs/screenshots/login-flow.png.

expectations.text: Output mentions Slack and/or Jira as sources of information
expectations.evidence: Data note at top explicitly states: 'The Slack channel #auth-migration was not found' and 'the Jira project AUTH returned no issues'.

expectations.text: Output uses Slack emoji section format (✅, 🔄, 👀 or similar)
expectations.evidence: Uses ✅ *Done*, 🔄 *In Progress*, 🚧 *Blockers*, 👀 *Up Next* with Slack single-asterisk bold.

expectations.text: Missing screenshot file is handled gracefully — noted for manual attachment rather than causing an error or being silently dropped
expectations.evidence: 'docs/screenshots/login-flow.png — file not found on disk; attach manually when posting to #product-team.'

expectations.text: Output is addressed to the correct audience context (PM Sarah, #product-team)
expectations.evidence: Screenshot note mentions #product-team. Data note references the channel context. Update is scoped for the right audience.

expectations.text: Output uses Slack-style bold (*word*) rather than markdown ## headers
expectations.evidence: Uses *Done*, *In Progress*, *Up Next* with single asterisks. No ## headers present.

expectations.text: Output contains at least one emoji section header (✅, 🔄, or 👀)
expectations.evidence: All three present: ✅ *Done*, 🔄 *In Progress*, 👀 *Up Next*

expectations.text: Output references specific git activity (real commit, feature, or fix — not generic placeholder text)
expectations.evidence: References WezTerm tab indicators, CWD fix, Claude Stop hook, create-draft-pr Playwright update, sandbox config, Obsidian frontmatter fix — all real commits.

expectations.text: Output is under 300 words
expectations.evidence: Output is approximately 130 words.

expectations.text: Output does not begin with meta-commentary like 'I'll write' or 'Here is your update'
expectations.evidence: Starts directly with '*Dotfiles — past 7 days*'

expectations.evidence: Uses ## markdown headers throughout (## Summary, ## Progress, ## Key Discussions, ## Metrics, ## Risks & Mitigations, ## Next 2 Weeks, ## Asks from PM). This is a comprehensive document, not a Slack message. Would render as raw markdown if pasted into Slack.

expectations.text: Missing screenshot file is handled gracefully — noted for manual attachment rather than causing an error or being silently dropped
expectations.evidence: 'The login-flow screenshot (docs/screenshots/login-flow.png) was not found. Please attach the latest screenshot from the design handoff or a staging environment recording before posting.'

expectations.evidence: Header explicitly states 'To: Sarah (PM)' and 'Channel: #product-team'.

expectations.text: Output uses Slack-style bold (*word*) rather than markdown ## headers
expectations.evidence: Uses **double-asterisk** bold throughout (e.g. **#platform-updates**, **Claude Code improvements**). Double asterisks don't render as bold in Slack — they show as literal asterisks or behave inconsistently.

expectations.evidence: Uses text section headers like **Claude Code improvements**, **WezTerm terminal**, **Minor**. No emoji headers present.
